My Analysis Title

  • Show All Code

Analysis
ggplot2
Modelling
One-sentence description of what this analysis investigates
Published

March 1, 2025

Question

State the question or problem this analysis addresses in 1–2 sentences.

Data

Describe the dataset and load it here.

Code 
library(tidyverse)
library(here)

# Load your data
# data <- read_csv(here("data", "your-data.csv"))

# For demo, use a built-in dataset
data <- palmerpenguins::penguins |> drop_na()

glimpse(data)
Rows: 333
Columns: 8
$ species           <fct> Adelie, Adelie, Adelie, Adelie, Adelie, Adelie, Adel…
$ island            <fct> Torgersen, Torgersen, Torgersen, Torgersen, Torgerse…
$ bill_length_mm    <dbl> 39.1, 39.5, 40.3, 36.7, 39.3, 38.9, 39.2, 41.1, 38.6…
$ bill_depth_mm     <dbl> 18.7, 17.4, 18.0, 19.3, 20.6, 17.8, 19.6, 17.6, 21.2…
$ flipper_length_mm <int> 181, 186, 195, 193, 190, 181, 195, 182, 191, 198, 18…
$ body_mass_g       <int> 3750, 3800, 3250, 3450, 3650, 3625, 4675, 3200, 3800…
$ sex               <fct> male, female, female, female, male, female, male, fe…
$ year              <int> 2007, 2007, 2007, 2007, 2007, 2007, 2007, 2007, 2007…

Exploration

Code 
data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = bill_length_mm, fill = species)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#1B6CA8", "#E26A2C", "#2CA85A")) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Bill Length by Species",
    x = "Bill length (mm)",
    y = "Count",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

Analysis

Describe your approach, then show the code.

Code 
model <- lm(body_mass_g ~ bill_length_mm + flipper_length_mm + species,
            data = data)

broom::tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE) |>
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") |>
  knitr::kable(digits = 2, caption = "Model coefficients")
Model coefficients
term estimate std.error statistic p.value conf.low conf.high
bill_length_mm 60.12 7.21 8.34 0.00 45.94 74.30
flipper_length_mm 27.54 3.21 8.58 0.00 21.23 33.86
speciesChinstrap -732.42 82.06 -8.93 0.00 -893.85 -570.98
speciesGentoo 113.25 89.21 1.27 0.21 -62.23 288.74

Key Findings

  • Finding 1 — one sentence explaining what you found
  • Finding 2 — one sentence
  • Finding 3 — one sentence

Conclusion

Summarise the takeaways in a short paragraph. Link to any follow-up work if relevant.

Source code: GitHub

Source Code
---
title: "My Analysis Title"
description: "One-sentence description of what this analysis investigates"
date: 2025-03-01
categories: [Analysis, ggplot2, Modelling]
image: "../assets/thumbnails/analysis-one.png"  # optional
toc: true
code-fold: true
---

## Question

State the question or problem this analysis addresses in 1–2 sentences.

## Data

Describe the dataset and load it here.

```{r}
#| label: load-data
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)
library(here)

# Load your data
# data <- read_csv(here("data", "your-data.csv"))

# For demo, use a built-in dataset
data <- palmerpenguins::penguins |> drop_na()

glimpse(data)
```

## Exploration

```{r}
#| label: eda
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of key variable"

data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = bill_length_mm, fill = species)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#1B6CA8", "#E26A2C", "#2CA85A")) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Bill Length by Species",
    x = "Bill length (mm)",
    y = "Count",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
```

## Analysis

Describe your approach, then show the code.

```{r}
#| label: model

model <- lm(body_mass_g ~ bill_length_mm + flipper_length_mm + species,
            data = data)

broom::tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE) |>
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") |>
  knitr::kable(digits = 2, caption = "Model coefficients")
```

## Key Findings

- **Finding 1** — one sentence explaining what you found
- **Finding 2** — one sentence
- **Finding 3** — one sentence

## Conclusion

Summarise the takeaways in a short paragraph. Link to any follow-up work if relevant.

---
